summary: Session 2 (last): live >32 KiB smoke probe + release staging for the Copilot-CLI large-prompt handoff. Step 1 auth-preflight PASSED (binary + credential + live probe on claude-sonnet-4.6, session c2bf12af, 1 premium req) -> seat viable. Step 2 live probe through the installed Copilot CLI 1.0.69 on Windows via CopilotCliTransport.dispatch: (a) an 81,829-char composed prompt (rendered inline 81,925 UTF-16 units, far past Windows' 32,767 ceiling) took the handoff pull path -- three unguessable facts at offsets 559/41,194/81,820 were ALL fused into the single required answer line (proving an end-to-end read of the 82,231-byte payload file), nonce EOF ack validated and stripped, handoff=true, payload_file_modified=false, 1 premium req; (b) a below-threshold control took the inline path (handoff=false). The transport surfaced NO defects (fix loop a no-op). Evidence: s2-live-probe.md + s2-live-probe-raw.json. Step 4 release staging: pyproject.toml + ai_router/__init__.py __version__ -> 0.34.0; CHANGELOG 0.34.0 finalized. Full pytest 3055 passed/6 skipped; guidance ceilings OK; advisory path-aware critique clean (google + anthropic, 0 findings). VERIFICATION: round-1 discovery (gpt-5-6, 2/2 fan-out) returned ISSUES_FOUND with one real Major (both fan-out calls) -- the probe ran on CLI 1.0.69 while copilot-catalog.lock pins 1.0.68 (cli_version_pin_required=true), a runtime-fail-closed pin (validate_catalog in route() copilot-cli setup) that blocks Set 103's copilot-cli-profile walk, and the evidence overclaimed 'through the pinned CLI'. Finding ACCEPTED (not disputed): corrected every overclaim across s2-live-probe.md + CHANGELOG (verifier's accepted option iii), disclosed the drift as a first-class Known Issue (copilot-cli profile only -- not api, not the package publish). Round-2 remediation-review (gpt-5-6) accepted the correction delta (VERIFIED, 1 fix accepted). The close BACKSTOP (round 3, gpt-5-6, full-diff vs the S1 baseline) independently RE-FOUND the pinned-CLI acceptance-gate gap -- expected, because it is real and unresolvable in code (the seat is 1.0.69, not the pinned 1.0.68). OPERATOR ADJUDICATION (two AskUserQuestion decisions, logged): (1) ship 0.34.0 now, reconcile the pin as part of Set 103 seat prep; (2) close via the attested operator override (--manual-verify) and push v0.34.0 to PyPI. Verdict WAIVED: a real Major, operator-waived for this release, disclosed as a Known Issue, with the pin reconciliation carried as a new blocking Set-103 precondition. Attestation: s2-manual-verify-attestation.md. Premium requests spent: 3 (preflight + 2 probes), all operator-sanctioned.
